questions: You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. You just got a call that your car needs a $3,000 repair. How do you react?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. Your boss tells you there are going to be layoffs next month. What's going through your mind?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. You find out a close friend has been talking behind your back. How do you respond?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. Your flight just got cancelled and you're stuck at the airport overnight. What do you do?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. You bombed an important exam you studied hard for. How do you feel?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. Your landlord just raised your rent by 25%. What's your reaction?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. A coworker takes credit for your idea in a meeting. How do you handle it?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. You just got a parking ticket on top of a bad day. What goes through your head?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. Your doctor calls with some test results they want to discuss in person. How do you react?
You're someone who takes things seriously and engages emotionally with problems. You spill coffee on your laptop right before a deadline. What do you do?
